<!-- Generated by spec-lite | agent: plan_feature | date: 2026-05-31 -->

# Feature: Request Processing Enhancements

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-RPE-001
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Strengthen Wave Client's core request-processing experience ahead of release as a
prominent web request client. Four independent enhancements:

1. **"Sent" view** — A new request-side tab that shows the *actual* HTTP request
   that left the client (final resolved URL, all headers actually sent, and the
   request body), so users can confirm exactly what their `{{env-variables}}`,
   auth, and cookies resolved to. Ephemeral (in the global store, never persisted),
   displayed in a small/subtle font with collapsible sections.
2. **Default User-Agent** — Inject an RFC 7231-compliant Wave Client `User-Agent`
   header on outgoing HTTP requests when the user has not supplied one.
3. **Move request** — Move a request item from one collection/folder to another,
   reusing the `RequestSaveWizard` modal (retitled) with a "cannot move to the
   same path" guard.
4. **Duplicate request** — Duplicate a request item in place with a fresh id and a
   unique `" Copy"`-suffixed name; a full deep copy of the original.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, adapter pattern, Result pattern, Zustand slice
> pattern, Vitest + RTL, JSDoc) are in `.spec-lite/memory.md`. Only additions /
> overrides specific to this work are listed here.

### Approach

- **Service-level capture (chosen).** The "Sent" snapshot is taken inside
  `HttpService.execute()` immediately before the `axios(...)` call — after cookies,
  auth headers, query params, content-type adjustments, and the new default
  User-Agent have all been applied. This is the most truthful representation of
  what hit the wire. Both platforms (VS Code, Web) already funnel sends through
  `httpService.execute()` and return via `handleHttpResponse(tabId, response.value)`,
  so no new message plumbing is required — the snapshot rides the existing
  `HttpResponseResult` back to the store.
- **Show secrets in full (chosen).** Headers such as `Authorization`, API keys, and
  `Cookie` are displayed verbatim (like Postman). The data is in-memory only and
  must never be persisted or logged. See Cross-Cutting Concerns → Security.
- **HTTP-only (chosen).** This pass captures "Sent" data for HTTP tabs only.
  WebSocket and SSE are deferred (logged in `TODO.md`). `SseService.ts` and
  `WebSocketService.ts` are **not** modified by this feature.
- **Reuse over rebuild for Move.** Move and Duplicate are composed from the
  existing `IStorageAdapter` methods (`saveRequestToCollection`,
  `deleteRequestFromCollection`) — no new adapter methods are introduced.
- **Tab name: "Sent".**

### Spec-Specific Overrides

- The `RealtimeTabState` invariant "must not contain auth credentials, tokens, or
  resolved secret headers" (`packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts`) is **not**
  touched — because "Sent" is HTTP-only and stored on `TabData`, not in the
  realtime slice. No override needed. (If WS/SSE is built later, that decision must
  be revisited.)
- "Show secrets in full" is a deliberate, scoped relaxation of the general "never
  expose secrets in UI" instinct, justified because the data is ephemeral
  (in-memory, non-persisted, non-logged) and the whole purpose is request
  debugging. Documented explicitly so reviewers don't flag it as a regression.

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

> No persisted/relational data model changes. `.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not
> exist. All new state is ephemeral in the Zustand store.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`SentRequestData`** (new type — `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts`):
  - `method` (string) — final HTTP method.
  - `url` (string) — final absolute URL **including** the resolved query string.
  - `headers` (`Record<string, string>`) — every header actually sent (post auth,
    cookies, content-type, User-Agent). Multi-value headers joined with `, `.
  - `body` (`SentRequestBody | undefined`) — display-safe body representation
    (undefined when no body was sent).
- **`SentRequestBody`** (new — same file): a single minimal shape, not a union.
  - `{ text: string; format: SentRequestBodyFormat }`
  - `SentRequestBodyFormat = 'json' | 'xml' | 'html' | 'text' | 'csv'` (mirrors the
    raw-body languages in `RequestBody.tsx`); the UI formats/highlights by hint.
  - Form-data and url-encoded bodies are JSON-encoded (`format: 'json'`); file/binary
    bodies are summarised to metadata text (`format: 'text'`); raw bodies keep their
    text with `format` derived from the Content-Type. Never carries base64/binary
    payloads. Content-Type is not surfaced in the Body section (only in Headers).

### Relationships

- `TabData` 1:0..1 `SentRequestData` via a new nullable `sentRequest` field
  (ephemeral; replaced on each send, reset on new send / clear / load).

### Indexes & Constraints

- N/A (in-memory only). `sentRequest` MUST default to `null` in `createEmptyTab()`
  and MUST be reset to `null` when a new request starts.

## 4. Files

**Slice 1 — "Sent" view**

- `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts` — add `SentRequestData`,
  `SentRequestBody`; add optional `sentRequest?: SentRequestData` to `ResponseData`.
- `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` — add optional
  `sentRequest?: SentRequestData` to `HttpResponseResult`.
- `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts` — add `sentRequest: SentRequestData | null` to
  `TabData`; default `null` in `createEmptyTab()`; add `'Sent'` to
  `RequestSectionTab`; include `'Sent'` in `getRequestTabsForProtocol('http')` only.
- `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createRequestTabsSlice.tsx` — `handleHttpResponse`
  sets `sentRequest` from the response; `setTabProcessingState` and `clearTab` /
  `clearActiveTab` reset it to `null`.
- `packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts` — build and return the
  `SentRequestData` snapshot (normal, error, and Digest-internal return paths).
- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestSent.tsx` — **new** core component
  (collapsible URL / Headers / Body sections, subtle styling).
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — export `RequestSent`.
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx` — render `RequestSent` for
  `activeRequestSection === 'Sent'`.
- `packages/web/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx` — same.

**Slice 2 — Default User-Agent**

- `packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts` — inject default User-Agent in
  header normalization when absent.
- `packages/shared/src/services/constants.ts` (or nearest existing constants
  module) — `WAVE_CLIENT_USER_AGENT` constant.

**Slice 3 — Move request**

- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestSaveWizard.tsx` — add
  `mode: 'save' | 'move'` prop (title, description, hide request-name field for
  move, optional prefill).
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionTreeItem.tsx` — add "Move" menu
  item (request items only) + `onMoveItem` prop.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx` — move-modal state,
  `handleMoveItem`, same-path validation, two-collection store update.

**Slice 4 — Duplicate request**

- `packages/core/src/utils/collectionParser.ts` — add `generateUniqueCopyName()`
  and `duplicateRequestItem()` helpers.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionTreeItem.tsx` — add "Duplicate"
  menu item (request items only) + `onDuplicateItem` prop.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx` — `handleDuplicateItem`.

**Tests**

- `packages/core/src/test/...` mirroring the above (see each task's Unit Tests).
- `packages/shared/src/...` HttpService tests for snapshot + User-Agent (use the
  package's existing test setup).

## 5. Dependencies

- None new. Reuses `axios`, Zustand store, Radix accordion/dropdown, existing
  `IStorageAdapter` methods, and `lucide-react` icons (no new icon library).

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add `SentRequestData` types and wire ephemeral `sentRequest` through the store

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Add `SentRequestData` and `SentRequestBody` to
    `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts`. Add optional
    `sentRequest?: SentRequestData` to `ResponseData` (same file) and to
    `HttpResponseResult` in `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts`.
  - Add `sentRequest: SentRequestData | null` to `TabData`
    (`packages/core/src/types/tab.ts`); set it to `null` in `createEmptyTab()`.
  - In `createRequestTabsSlice.tsx`: in `handleHttpResponse`, set
    `sentRequest: (response as ResponseData).sentRequest ?? null` alongside the
    existing `responseData` update. In `setTabProcessingState`, when
    `isProcessing === true`, also reset `sentRequest: null`. In `clearTab` /
    `clearActiveTab`, `sentRequest` resets via `createEmptyTab()` defaults.
  - Export the new types from `packages/core/src/index.ts` if not re-exported.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Store test (`src/test/hooks/createRequestTabsSlice` area) —
  `handleHttpResponse` stores `sentRequest`; starting a new send via
  `setTabProcessingState(tabId, true)` clears it; a response without `sentRequest`
  leaves it `null`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `SentRequestData`, `SentRequestBody`, and
  the new `TabData.sentRequest` field (note: ephemeral, never persisted).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test` passes; a synthetic
  `handleHttpResponse` call surfaces `getActiveTab().sentRequest`.

### TASK-002: Capture the "Sent" snapshot in `HttpService.execute()`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts`, build a
  `SentRequestData` from the final `requestUrl` (with params), final `headers`, and
  a display-safe body derived from `request.body`:
  - serialized formdata → `{ kind: 'formdata', fields: [...] }` (key, type, and for
    text the value; for file the `fileName`/`contentType` — never the base64 data);
  - serialized file → `{ kind: 'file', fileName, contentType }`;
  - `Record<string,string>` (urlencoded) → `{ kind: 'urlencoded', text: 'k=v&...' }`;
  - string → `{ kind: 'raw', text, contentType: <content-type header> }`;
  - null/undefined → `undefined`.
  Return `sentRequest` on all three return paths: the normal success return, the
  Digest `handledInternally` return, and the `catch` error return. Capture the
  snapshot at the point the wire values are final (after auth/cookies/content-type).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: HttpService tests (mock axios) — assert `result.sentRequest`
  contains the cookie header, applied auth header, final URL with params, and the
  correct body `kind`; assert the error path still returns `sentRequest`; assert
  file/formdata bodies never include base64 payloads.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the snapshot helper and an inline comment
  explaining *why* capture happens immediately before `axios(...)`.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test` (or package equivalent)
  passes; snapshot reflects post-auth headers.

### TASK-003: Inject a default Wave Client `User-Agent`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `WAVE_CLIENT_USER_AGENT` (e.g.,
  `` `WaveClient/${version}` ``, version sourced from the package/build constant
  with a hard-coded fallback) to a shared constants module. In
  `HttpService.execute()` header handling, add it **only** when no existing header
  matches `user-agent` case-insensitively. Applies to `execute()`; the internal
  `send()` path may reuse the same helper.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: HttpService tests — default UA added when absent; a
  user-supplied `User-Agent` (any casing) is preserved and not overridden; the UA
  appears in the captured `sentRequest.headers` (integration with TASK-002).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc/comment noting RFC 7231 `User-Agent` format
  and the "don't override user-set" rule.
- **Verify**: A request with no UA shows `WaveClient/<version>` in the Sent tab; a
  request with a custom UA shows the custom value.

### TASK-004: Add the "Sent" request tab type, gating, and `RequestSent` component

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `'Sent'` to `RequestSectionTab` and include it in
  `getRequestTabsForProtocol('http')` (HTTP only; not WS/SSE). Create
  `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestSent.tsx`: reads
  `useAppStateStore(state => state.getActiveTab()?.sentRequest)`; renders three
  collapsible sections (Radix accordion) — **URL** (method + full URL incl. query
  params on a **single line**, method styled via the shared `getHttpMethodColor`
  palette), **Headers** (key/value rows), **Body** (rendered from `text` + `format`
  hint; shows the format hint, no Content-Type row). Sections use **distinct
  header/content backgrounds** (header `bg-slate-100`/`dark:bg-slate-800`, content
  `bg-white`/`dark:bg-slate-900`) for a clear visual cue. Use `text-xs`, subtle
  colors, monospace for values, light/dark parity. Empty state when `sentRequest`
  is null: "Send the request to see exactly what was sent." Export from
  `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `RequestSent` renders URL/headers/body from a mock
  `sentRequest`; shows empty state when null; sections expand/collapse; formdata/file
  bodies render a field/file summary (no base64).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `RequestSent`; note it is HTTP-only and
  reads ephemeral store state.
- **Verify**: `getRequestTabsForProtocol('http')` includes `'Sent'`;
  `getRequestTabsForProtocol('ws'|'sse')` does not.

### TASK-005: Wire `RequestSent` into both Request editors

- **Verify**: Manually (or via test) the "Sent" tab appears after Params/Headers/

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `CollectionTreeItem.tsx`, add an `onMoveItem(item,

### TASK-008: Implement the Move handler with same-path validation in `CollectionsPane`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `CollectionsPane.tsx`, hold move state (source
  collection, parent path, item). Render `RequestSaveWizard` in `mode="move"`. On
  confirm: resolve destination collection name → filename via `collections`;
  compute destination `folderPath`. **Validate**: reject when destination
  collection+path equals the source collection+path (identical
  `collection/folder`); allow moving to a deeper child path or a different
  collection. On valid move: `storageAdapter.saveRequestToCollection(destFilename,
  destPath, item)` then `storageAdapter.deleteRequestFromCollection(srcFilename,
  srcPath, item.id)`, checking `result.isOk` per memory's Result-pattern rule;
  update the store for both affected collections via `updateCollection`. Surface
  failures with `notification.showNotification('error', ...)`. If save succeeds but
  delete fails, notify and do not remove from source (avoid data loss / duplicate
  silently — report it).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Same-path move is blocked with an error notification;
  move-to-child-folder is allowed; cross-collection move calls save then delete and
  updates both collections; save-failure aborts before delete.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `handleMoveItem`; note the same-path rule
  and the save-then-delete ordering.
- **Verify**: Moving `collection1/folder1` → `collection1/folder1` is rejected;
  `collection1/folder1` → `collection1/folder1/child` succeeds.

### TASK-009: Implement Duplicate (request-only) with unique-name deep copy

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add to `collectionParser.ts`:
  `generateUniqueCopyName(baseName, siblings)` → `"<name> Copy"`, then
  `"<name> Copy 2"`, … avoiding case-insensitive sibling collisions; and
  `duplicateRequestItem(item)` → deep clone with a fresh `crypto.randomUUID()` for
  the item id (and the nested `request.id` if present), copying everything else.
  Add `onDuplicateItem(item, parentItemPath)` prop + "Duplicate" `DropdownMenuItem`
  (request menu only) in `CollectionTreeItem.tsx`. In `CollectionsPane.tsx`, add
  `handleDuplicateItem`: build the copy via the helpers using
  `getSiblingsAtPath(collection.item, parentItemPath)` for the unique name, then
  `storageAdapter.saveRequestToCollection(filename, parentItemPath, copy)` and
  `updateCollection` on success; error via notification on failure.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `generateUniqueCopyName` returns `"Req Copy"`, then
  `"Req Copy 2"` on collision; `duplicateRequestItem` produces a new id/request.id
  and otherwise-identical content; duplicate is offered for requests only (not
  folders); handler saves into the same path and updates the store.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on both new utils and the `onDuplicateItem`
  prop with an `@example`.
- **Verify**: Duplicating a request creates a sibling named "<name> Copy" with a new
  id; duplicating again yields "<name> Copy 2"; folders have no Duplicate option.

- [ ] **Implementation**: Ensure all new public types, components, hooks-store

  "Sent" tab, default User-Agent, Move, and Duplicate.

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: The "Sent" view shows resolved auth headers (e.g., `Authorization`) in
  full, by explicit decision. The default User-Agent never overrides a user-set
  header and is auth-agnostic.
- **Error Handling**: All adapter calls (move/duplicate) follow the Result pattern —
  check `result.isOk`, surface errors via `notification.showNotification('error', …)`,
  never silently swallow. HttpService capture must not change request success/failure
  semantics — it only attaches metadata and must be exception-safe (a snapshot
  failure must never break the send).
- **Logging**: Do **not** log `SentRequestData` (it can contain secrets). No new
  logging is added for the Sent view.
- **Security**: `SentRequestData` is in-memory only on `TabData` — never written to
  storage, history, collections, or logs. It must be reset on new send and dropped
  on tab clear/close. Reviewers: the verbatim secret display is intentional and
  scoped to this ephemeral view.
- **Persistence**: Move/Duplicate persist via `IStorageAdapter` and update the
  Zustand store to keep the collections tree in sync without a full reload.
